behavior: Diurnal fish-eating raptor; searches over water, may hover briefly, then dives feet-first to seize fish.
field_marks: Slender, large raptor with long, narrow wings and long legs
Dark brown upperparts sharply contrast with mostly white underparts
White head marked by a broad dark-brown stripe through each eye
Black, strongly hooked bill; pale blue-gray feet with long curved talons
In flight, wings show a distinct wrist kink forming an M-shape from below
Pale underwings show dark wrist patches and barred flight feathers
habitat: Freshwater, coastal, and brackish waters with abundant fish and safe open nesting structures; occurs around lakes, rivers, ponds, marshes, estuaries, reservoirs, and reefs.

sources.title: Osprey Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
